info.title: InferSports API
info.description: Sharp Asian odds for agents — live football & basketball: Asian-handicap (quarter lines with win / half-win / push), de-vigged sharp (Pinnacle) fair probabilities, live scores, opening lines, and value detection. Read-only. Keyless Free tier; an optional isk_ key raises limits and unlocks the sharp book.

parameters.name: league

schema.description: league id (lg_…) or name (fuzzy-matched, e.g. 'World Cup')
schema.title: League

parameters.description: league id (lg_…) or name (fuzzy-matched, e.g. 'World Cup')

parameters.name: date

schema.description: calendar date YYYY-MM-DD (UTC day unless timezone given)
schema.title: Date

parameters.description: calendar date YYYY-MM-DD (UTC day unless timezone given)

parameters.name: timezone

schema.description: IANA timezone for the date's day boundary (e.g. Asia/Shanghai); default UTC
schema.title: Timezone

parameters.description: IANA timezone for the date's day boundary (e.g. Asia/Shanghai); default UTC

get.description:
Opening odds (初盘) for this match's currently-visible quotes.

For each current quote the caller may see, returns the upstream's frozen first-seen price at the
same bookmaker / market / period / line — compare against ``/odds`` to read line movement. 1x2
yields one opening per book; totals/AH yield the opening at each current line. Best-effort: a
bookmaker/line with no opening on file is omitted (the lookup is a single-point, fail-open call).

parameters.name: league

schema.description: case-insensitive substring on the league name
schema.title: League

parameters.description: case-insensitive substring on the league name

get.description: Discovery manifest of the available MCP tools.

schema.title: Response List Tools V1 Mcp Tools Get

get.description:
Request / error / latency metrics for the CALLER'S OWN key (PRD-v3 §7).

Requires a real API key — keyless callers are rejected. Scoped to the caller: only this key's
own bucket is returned (masked), never the full per-key map, so one customer can't read another
customer's key material or usage. The session-scoped dashboard uses /platform/usage instead.

bookmaker.title: Bookmaker
bookmaker.description: Book offering the best price for this leg (its key).

price.title: Price
price.description: Best price for this leg, in the requested format.

legs.title: Legs
legs.description: Best book per outcome — one leg per outcome.

ArbOpportunity.title: ArbOpportunity
ArbOpportunity.description:
A cross-book price inefficiency: best price per outcome (same line) inverts to < 1.

DETECTION ONLY — we report the margin and which book holds each leg; we do NOT size stakes or
link out to place bets (InferSports is read-only).

price.title: Price
price.description: Best price for this outcome in the requested format.

decimal.title: Decimal
decimal.description: Same price as decimal odds (for comparison).

overround.title: Overround
overround.description: Book margin = Σ(1/decimal) − 1 over this market's outcomes.

note.title: Note
note.description: Tier coverage caveat — e.g. Free tier excludes sharp books; null-safe string.

format.title: Format
format.description: Display format for prices (analytics use decimal).

verbosity.title: Verbosity
verbosity.description: full | terse. 'terse' drops the per-book `books` array (the bulk of the payload) and returns only the computed answer (best_prices / consensus_line / fair_odds / summary). Use it when you only need the worked numbers, not every book's line-by-line quote.

stale.title: Stale
stale.description: True when as_of is older than the freshness window for the match state.

consensus_line.title: Consensus Line
consensus_line.description: Line offered by the most books (null for 1x2 / no books).

best_prices.title: Best Prices
best_prices.description: Best price per outcome on the consensus line (across all returned books).

fair_odds.title: Fair Odds
fair_odds.description: De-vigged fair decimal odds from the sharpest (lowest-overround) book on the consensus line.

fair_from.title: Fair From
fair_from.description: Bookmaker key the fair_odds were de-vigged from.

books.title: Books
books.description: Every book's quote for this market, line-by-line. Empty when verbosity='terse' (check book_count for how many books backed the analytics — empty here ≠ no books).

summary.title: Summary
summary.description: One ready-to-read sentence stating the consensus line, best prices and fair odds (W2) — quote it to the user directly. Present in both verbosity modes.

query.title: Query
query.description: Natural-language fixture, e.g. 'France vs Argentina' or a team.

external_prob.title: External Prob
external_prob.description: The external (e.g. prediction-market) implied probability for `outcome`, in (0,1). Pre-net it for the venue's fee/spread (we don't ingest prediction-market data).

outcome.title: Outcome
outcome.description: Which leg the external_prob is for: home/draw/away (1x2), home/away (asian_handicap), over/under (totals).

external_label.title: External Label
external_label.description: Cosmetic source label echoed back, e.g. 'polymarket' | 'kalshi'.

date.title: Date
date.description: Optional UTC date YYYY-MM-DD to disambiguate.

fair_decimal.title: Fair Decimal
fair_decimal.description: Same fair value as decimal odds.

fair_from.title: Fair From
fair_from.description: Bookmaker key the fair line was de-vigged from.

edge_pp.title: Edge Pp
edge_pp.description: (fair_prob − external_prob)·100, in percentage points. Positive ⇒ external YES is cheap.

ev_roi.title: Ev Roi
ev_roi.description: Expected ROI buying YES at external_prob = edge/external_prob.

verdict.title: Verdict
verdict.description: good | marginal | no_edge (vs a market-shape noise floor).

caveats.title: Caveats
caveats.description: Mapping/freshness traps to respect before acting.

CompareProbResult.title: CompareProbResult
CompareProbResult.description:
Is an external probability good vs our sharp de-vigged fair line? DETECTION ONLY (read-only).

``status``: ``ok`` (fair line found, edge computed), ``no_line`` (no sharp fair for that
market/period — can't judge), ``ambiguous`` (alternatives + ask_user; never guessed), or
``not_found``. ``edge_pp = (fair_prob − external_prob)·100`` (positive ⇒ the external YES price is
cheap vs the sharp fair). The caller owns the prediction-market→probability mapping; ``caveats``
flags the traps (1x2 regulation/draw, AH push mass, staleness).

bookmaker.title: Bookmaker
bookmaker.description: Optional bookmaker key (e.g. 'crown') whose Malay-odds rounding to apply; omitted → canonical rounding.

safe_to_proceed.title: Safe To Proceed
safe_to_proceed.description: True when the result is unambiguous enough to act on without asking the user.

ask_user.title: Ask User
ask_user.description: A disambiguation question to put to the user; null when safe_to_proceed.

next_action.description: Suggested next call; null when the caller must ask first or nothing follows.

Decision.title: Decision
Decision.description:
Pre-computed go/no-go for the caller (W3): we decide whether it's safe to proceed so the
agent doesn't have to reason it out (and risk guessing). Branch on ``safe_to_proceed``:
true → act on the result without asking; false → put ``ask_user`` to the human, or follow
``next_action`` when there's a clear recovery step (e.g. nothing resolved → list fixtures).

line.title: Line
line.description: Asian handicap line, a multiple of 0.25 (e.g. -0.75, 0, +1.5).

team.title: Team
team.description: Team name for home/away; null for a draw.

win_probability.title: Win Probability
win_probability.description: De-vigged implied probability for this outcome (0–1).

source.title: Source
source.description: Bookmaker key the 1x2 was de-vigged from.

Favorite.title: Favorite
Favorite.description: Who the market favors, in plain terms — de-vigged from the full-time 1x2 line.

markets.title: Markets
markets.description: Filter to these markets (1x2 / asian_handicap / totals); default all.

period.title: Period
period.description: Filter to full_time | half_time; default both.

min_margin_pct.title: Min Margin Pct
min_margin_pct.description: Only report opportunities whose guaranteed margin is at least this %%. Default 0 (any positive arb).

stale.title: Stale
stale.description: Freshness of the odds (W4); null if no match.

FindArbitrageResult.title: FindArbitrageResult
FindArbitrageResult.description:
One-call NL→arb scan: cross-book price inefficiencies within a fixture.

DETECTION ONLY (read-only): margin + which book holds each leg; never stakes or bet links.
``status`` is the same go/no-go as get_sharp_line (ok | ambiguous | not_found; never guessed).
Free tier drops the sharp book, which both shrinks the book set and removes the most common
arb counterparty — expect fewer hits.

query.title: Query
query.description: Natural-language fixture, e.g. 'Man City vs Arsenal' or a single team name.

date.title: Date
date.description: Optional UTC date 'YYYY-MM-DD' to disambiguate same-name fixtures.

decision.description: Pre-computed go/no-go (W3): safe_to_proceed, ask_user, and the suggested next_action.

markets.title: Markets
markets.description: Filter to these markets (1x2 / asian_handicap / totals); default all.

period.title: Period
period.description: Filter to full_time | half_time; default both.

min_edge_pct.title: Min Edge Pct
min_edge_pct.description: Only report outcomes whose best price beats the de-vigged fair price by at least this %% (edge = best/fair − 1). Default 1.0.

fair_source.title: Fair Source
fair_source.description: Bookmaker key whose de-vigged line was used as the fair reference.

note.title: Note
note.description: Caveats (e.g. Free tier: no sharp book, fair is approximate).

stale.title: Stale
stale.description: Freshness of the odds (W4); null if no match.

FindValueResult.title: FindValueResult
FindValueResult.description:
One-call NL→value scan: where does a book beat the sharp de-vigged fair line?

DETECTION ONLY (read-only): we surface the edge, not stakes or bet links. ``status`` is the
same go/no-go as get_sharp_line (ok | ambiguous | not_found; ambiguous never guessed). Requires
a sharp reference to de-vig — on the Free tier the sharp book (Pinnacle) is gated, so fair odds
fall back to the tightest available book and ``note`` flags the reduced confidence.

markets.title: Markets
markets.description: Filter to these market types: 1x2 | asian_handicap | totals.

bookmakers.title: Bookmakers
bookmakers.description: Filter to these bookmaker keys, e.g. ['pinnacle','crown'].

format.title: Format
format.description: Odds format: decimal | hk | malay | american | indonesian | probability.

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: Echo of the requested event id.

result.description: The cached result, or null when not in the 30-day cache.

GetResultResult.title: GetResultResult
GetResultResult.description: One finished-match result, or status='not_found' when it isn't in the cache.

ListBookmakersRequest.title: ListBookmakersRequest
ListBookmakersRequest.description: No parameters — the catalogue is fixed; tier coverage is applied from the caller's key.

date.title: Date
date.description: Calendar day to list, 'YYYY-MM-DD'. REQUIRED — this tool always returns a bounded window (use list_today_matches for 'today + still-live').

date_to.title: Date To
date_to.description: Optional inclusive end day 'YYYY-MM-DD' — one call covers the whole [date, date_to] window (max 31 days), e.g. a tournament's group stage. Omit for a single day.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: Optional IANA timezone (e.g. 'Asia/Shanghai', 'America/New_York'). The day boundary is computed in this zone; default is the UTC day. The response keeps UTC `scheduled_at` as canonical and adds `scheduled_at_local` per fixture.

date.title: Date
date.description: The calendar day listed (YYYY-MM-DD), in `timezone` if given else UTC.

date_to.title: Date To
date_to.description: Inclusive end day when a range was requested (null = single day).

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: Echo of the requested timezone (null = UTC day).

truncated.title: Truncated
truncated.description: True when more fixtures matched the day than were returned.

ListEventsResult.title: ListEventsResult
ListEventsResult.description:
A specific calendar day's fixtures (kickoff order), bounded by `limit`.

UTC-canonical: `date` echoes the requested day, `timezone` the zone its boundary was computed in
(null = UTC). Each fixture's `scheduled_at` stays UTC; `scheduled_at_local` is populated when a
timezone was given. `truncated` is true when more fixtures fell on the day than were returned —
narrow with sport/status/league rather than paging.

date.title: Date
date.description: Filter by UTC kickoff date 'YYYY-MM-DD' (the day the match was played).

team.title: Team
team.description: Case-insensitive substring matched against either team name.

league.title: League
league.description: Case-insensitive substring matched against the league name.

note.title: Note
note.description: Set when 0 results but the queried fixture exists and hasn't finished: its current status, so an empty list isn't mistaken for 'no such match'.

ListResultsResult.title: ListResultsResult
ListResultsResult.description: Finished-match results from the 30-day cache, most-recent-first.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: Optional IANA timezone (e.g. 'America/New_York', 'Asia/Shanghai') to render each fixture's kickoff in its `summary` as local time; default UTC.

MatchAlternative.title: MatchAlternative
MatchAlternative.description: A runner-up fixture when a query is ambiguous.

query.title: Query
query.description: Natural-language fixture or team, e.g. 'Brazil vs Argentina' or just 'Brazil'.

timezone.title: Timezone
timezone.description: Optional IANA timezone (e.g. 'America/New_York', 'Asia/Shanghai') to show the kickoff time in local time; default UTC.

date.title: Date
date.description: Optional UTC date 'YYYY-MM-DD' to disambiguate same-name fixtures.

scheduled_at_local.title: Scheduled At Local
scheduled_at_local.description: Kickoff in the requested timezone (ISO-8601); null if no timezone given or unparseable.

implied_probabilities.title: Implied Probabilities
implied_probabilities.description: De-vigged win probabilities per 1x2 outcome (home/draw/away); null when no 1x2.

stale.title: Stale
stale.description: Freshness of the data (W4); null when no match resolved.

MatchInfoResult.title: MatchInfoResult
MatchInfoResult.description:
One-call NL→basics: score, status, kickoff (local), and who's favored — no betting knowledge needed.

The casual front door. ``status`` is the same go/no-go as get_sharp_line (ok | ambiguous |
not_found; ambiguous is never guessed). ``summary`` is a ready-to-read one-liner. ``favorite`` and
``implied_probabilities`` are best-effort — null when no 1x2 is on file (e.g. the sharp book is
gated on the Free tier and no other book quotes 1x2 for this fixture).

as_of.title: As Of
as_of.description: Freshness stamp — last upstream update applied (ISO-8601 UTC).

stale.title: Stale
stale.description: True when as_of is older than the freshness window for the match state (live vs pre-match) — warn the user before quoting these as live.

red_cards.description: Final per-side red-card counts {home, away}; null if none on file.

finished_at.title: Finished At
finished_at.description: When this result was first recorded as finished (ISO-8601 UTC).

event_id.title: Event Id
event_id.description: Canonical event id (evt_…); pass to get_match_odds / compare_lines.

scheduled_at_local.title: Scheduled At Local
scheduled_at_local.description: Kickoff in the requested timezone (ISO-8601); null when no timezone was given.

score.description: Current score when live, else null.

red_cards.description: Red cards per side; null when not tracked by upstream.

clock.title: Clock
clock.description: Match clock when live, humanized for display: e.g. '1st half 25'', '2nd half 47'' (the real minute, not estimated) or 'half-time'; null pre-match or when the feed omits it. Basketball clocks (e.g. 'Q2 4:12') pass through as-is.

summary.title: Summary
summary.description: One-line human-readable status, e.g. "Man City vs Arsenal — LIVE 1h 23 2-1." or "… — Kicks off 18:00 UTC on Jun 01." — ready to read to a user.

MatchSummary.title: MatchSummary
MatchSummary.description: A fixture flattened for agent consumption (teams as names, optional live score).

args.title: Args
args.description: Ready-to-use arguments for that call, e.g. {'event_id': 'evt_…'}.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Why this is the suggested next step.

NextAction.title: NextAction
NextAction.description: The single best next call to make, pre-computed so the agent doesn't have to plan it (W3).

line.title: Line
line.description: The OPENING line (null for 1x2). For totals/AH this is the line the market opened at; it may differ from `current_line` — that difference IS the line movement.

handicap_components.title: Handicap Components
handicap_components.description: Quarter-line split of the opening line, when applicable.

opening.title: Opening
opening.description: Opening (first-seen, frozen) prices in the requested format.

current.title: Current
current.description: Current prices (null if no current quote). 1x2 is same line; for totals/AH read alongside `current_line`, as the line may have moved since open — diff for movement.

current_line.title: Current Line
current_line.description: Line the CURRENT quote sits at (null for 1x2 or when no current quote). Differs from `line` when the market moved since open.

current_handicap_components.title: Current Handicap Components
current_handicap_components.description: Quarter-line split of the current line, when applicable.

opened_at.title: Opened At
opened_at.description: When the upstream first recorded this opening (ISO-8601 UTC).

OpeningLine.title: OpeningLine
OpeningLine.description: A book's opening (first-seen) quote paired with its current quote for movement reading.

query.title: Query
query.description: Natural-language fixture, e.g. 'Real Madrid vs Barcelona' or a single team name.

markets.title: Markets
markets.description: Filter to these market types: 1x2 | asian_handicap | totals.

bookmakers.title: Bookmakers
bookmakers.description: Filter to these bookmaker keys, e.g. ['pinnacle','crown'].

format.title: Format
format.description: Odds format: decimal | hk | malay | american | indonesian | probability.

date.title: Date
date.description: Optional UTC date 'YYYY-MM-DD' to disambiguate same-name fixtures.

stale.title: Stale
stale.description: Freshness of the current quotes (W4); null when no match resolved.

OpeningLineResult.title: OpeningLineResult
OpeningLineResult.description:
One-call NL→opening: resolve a fixture and return each book's opening line + current price.

``status``: ok | ambiguous | not_found (same go/no-go contract as get_sharp_line — never
guesses an ambiguous fixture). The opening is the **true market open** for every market, so
open→current is real movement: for **1x2** a price move; for **totals/AH** compare `line`
(opening) vs `current_line` to read the line move, and the prices alongside. Best-effort
upstream: a market with no opening yet on file is omitted.

OpeningQuote.title: OpeningQuote
OpeningQuote.description:
An opening (初盘) quote — the open-line counterpart of :class:`OddsQuote`.

Same shape as a current quote minus the live ``status`` (an opening is by definition the frozen
first-seen price). ``opened_at`` is when the upstream first recorded it (UTC). This is the **true
market opening**: for totals/AH the ``line`` is the *opening* line, which may differ from the
current ``/odds`` quote's line — diff price **and** line across the two endpoints to read movement.

red_cards.description: Final per-side red-card counts {home, away}; null if none on file.

finished_at.title: Finished At
finished_at.description: When this result was first recorded as finished (ISO-8601 UTC).

ResultEntry.title: ResultEntry
ResultEntry.description: A finished match's cached result — final score, red cards and when it finished.

note.title: Note
note.description: Set when 0 results but the queried fixture exists and hasn't finished: its current status, so an empty list isn't mistaken for 'no such match'.

markets.title: Markets
markets.description: Limit the value/arb scan to these markets; default all.

period.title: Period
period.description: Limit to full_time | half_time; default both.

min_edge_pct.title: Min Edge Pct
min_edge_pct.description: Value threshold for the per-match signal (default 1.0%).

min_margin_pct.title: Min Margin Pct
min_margin_pct.description: Arbitrage threshold for the per-match signal (default 0%).

only_signal.title: Only Signal
only_signal.description: If true, return only matches that have a value or arb signal.

count.title: Count
count.description: Total fixtures in the filtered slate (before limit).

with_signal.title: With Signal
with_signal.description: How many of those have a value or arb signal.

returned.title: Returned
returned.description: How many entries are in this response.

truncated.title: Truncated
truncated.description: True if more fixtures matched than were returned.

ScanSlateResult.title: ScanSlateResult
ScanSlateResult.description:
Today's slate with each fixture's honest status + value/arb signal, signal-first.

Collapses the per-fixture loop into one call. ``count`` is the whole filtered slate; entries are
sorted with value/arb matches first and truncated to ``limit`` (so truncation drops the quiet
ones). Line movement is NOT here (it needs the opening lookup) — drill into one fixture with
get_opening_line. DETECTION ONLY / read-only, like find_value & find_arbitrage.

prob.title: Prob
prob.description: Market-implied probability of that exact score.

query.title: Query
query.description: Natural-language fixture, e.g. 'Mexico vs South Africa' or a team.

top_cum.title: Top Cum
top_cum.description: Probability mass covered by the returned scores.

implied_1x2.title: Implied 1X2
implied_1x2.description: The grid's home/draw/away probabilities — the de-vigged 1x2 it was fit to, or, when the moneyline was suspended and supremacy came from the asian handicap, the 1x2 the fitted grid implies.

lambda_home.title: Lambda Home
lambda_home.description: Fitted Poisson goal rate, home (remaining goals when the match is live).

lambda_away.title: Lambda Away
lambda_away.description: Fitted Poisson goal rate, away (remaining goals when the match is live).

ah_ev_check.title: Ah Ev Check
ah_ev_check.description: EV of the fair asian-handicap price under the fitted grid (held-out cross-check; ≈0 means 1x2/totals/AH are internally consistent).

fair_from.title: Fair From
fair_from.description: Bookmaker whose de-vigged 1x2 anchored the fit.

ScoreProbResult.title: ScoreProbResult
ScoreProbResult.description:
Market-implied correct-score probabilities for ONE football match.

The de-vigged 1x2 + totals markets are inverted into a bivariate-Poisson
(Dixon-Coles) score grid; the asian-handicap fair price is held out and
reported as ``ah_ev_check`` (≈0 = the markets agree with the grid). When the
moneyline is suspended (common once a match is decided) but the books keep
totals + AH open, supremacy is solved from the handicap instead and
``ah_ev_check`` is null. Live matches are conditioned on the current score:
the lambdas describe REMAINING goals and scorelines are still full-time
(never below the current score). This is what the market implies, not a
house prediction. ``status`` mirrors get_sharp_line: ok | ambiguous |
not_found | no_data.

verbosity.title: Verbosity
verbosity.description: full | terse. 'terse' empties the per-book `books` array inside `comparison` (the bulk of the payload); the top-level `summary` and the worked numbers are kept either way.

stale.title: Stale
stale.description: Freshness of the comparison (W4); null when no match resolved.

decision.description: Pre-computed go/no-go (W3): safe_to_proceed + suggested next_action. Canonical home of the decision; the top-level ask_user is kept for back-compat.

SharpLineResult.title: SharpLineResult
SharpLineResult.description:
One-call NL→answer: resolve a fixture and return its worked sharp line (W1).

``status`` is the caller's go/no-go signal: ``ok`` (``match`` + ``comparison`` are set),
``ambiguous`` (``alternatives`` + ``ask_user`` are set, ``comparison`` is null — we do NOT
guess), or ``not_found``. ``summary`` is always a ready-to-read sentence.

status.title: Status
status.description: scheduled | live | finished (finished is inferred: elapsed-time backstop plus, for football, early detection when the match clock stops and every book pulls its quotes).

red_cards.description: Red cards per side; null when not tracked by upstream.

clock.title: Clock
clock.description: Match clock when live, humanized: e.g. '1st half 25'', '2nd half 47'' or 'half-time'; null otherwise.

book_count.title: Book Count
book_count.description: Distinct books quoting this fixture (after tier gating).

arb_count.title: Arb Count
arb_count.description: Number of cross-book arbitrage opportunities at/above min_margin_pct.

favorite.description: Who the full-time 1x2 line favors (de-vigged), e.g. {outcome:'home', team:'Brazil', win_probability:0.58}; null when no 1x2 is on file for this fixture/tier. Computed for the returned entries only.

signal.title: Signal
signal.description: One-line human summary: status + best value/arb (or 'no value/arb').

SlateEntry.title: SlateEntry
SlateEntry.description: One fixture on the slate with its honest status + pre-computed value/arb signal.

date.title: Date
date.description: The date treated as 'today' (YYYY-MM-DD) — UTC, or the given timezone's day.

note.title: Note
note.description: Set when the list is empty: the next upcoming fixture matching the filters.

bookmaker.title: Bookmaker
bookmaker.description: The book offering the value price (its key).

price.title: Price
price.description: Best available price, in the requested format.

fair_price.title: Fair Price
fair_price.description: De-vigged fair decimal price from the sharp reference book.

ValueBet.title: ValueBet
ValueBet.description: A single +EV outcome: a book's price exceeds the de-vigged fair price for that outcome.
